title: 3-ingredient banana bread – just too good
excerpt: I don't know what's gotten into me lately. I want to make food that's quick and delicious. I had a bunch of ripe bananas in my house and decided to tackle the little loaf. It really tastes too good. What gives the loaf its lovely flavour is the coconut cream in it …

seo.title: 3-ingredient banana bread - just too good - Marinda Kook

html:
I don't know what's gotten into me lately. I want to make food that's quick and delicious. I had a bunch of ripe bananas in my house and decided to tackle the little loaf. It really tastes too good. What gives the loaf its lovely flavour is the coconut cream in it. It's moist and you can taste the tropical bananas.

 

INGREDIENTS:

  • 6 ripe bananas
  • 500 g self-raising flour
  • 1 tin (400 ml) coconut cream

NOW:

  1. mash the bananas in your mixing bowl
  2. stir in the flour
  3. stir in the coconut cream
  4. grease your oven pan
  5. bake at 180 degrees Celsius for 60 minutes

I stirred a small amount (5ml) of ground cinnamon into my loaf. It just gives it a deeper flavour. And don't forget the pinch of salt!

Enjoy it with breakfast, along with tea, or just for the fun of it. A banana bread like this makes a wonderful bread-and-butter pudding.

You can dip slices of banana bread in egg and fry it for breakfast as French Toast.

It's so versatile. Simply enjoy it with a thick layer of butter!
